sound_type: loud 'godwit! godwit!' repeated -- nasal, two-syllable, insistent. Onomatopoeic origin of 'godwit.' Repeated 3-10+ times. E=5
ecosystem: Tidal flats, coastal mudflats, estuaries during migration and winter. Breeds on northern Great Plains grasslands.
season: Regular in PNW from fall through spring. Breeds on Great Plains. More common in PNW than Long-billed Curlew.

coordinates.description: Marbled Godwit on PNW coastal tidal flats. Regular fall through spring. Sweeping bill through mud and shallow water.

taxonomy_note.description: FIFTH SCOLOPACIDAE. Completes five-entry streak (168-172). Bill curves UP -- counter-curve to four degrees of down-curved bills. Bill morphology: straight > decurved > down-curved > extremely down-curved > UPTURNED. New genus: Limosa (godwits).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Scolopacidae: Wilson's Snipe (168), Dunlin (169), Whimbrel (170), Long-billed Curlew (171), Marbled Godwit (172).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Limosa from Latin 'limus' (mud) -- mud-dweller. fedoa possibly from Italian 'fedoa' -- name for godwit. Godwit from the call: 'godwit!' Marbled from the barred/marbled plumage pattern.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Limosa -- godwits. ~4 species. Large shorebirds with long, slightly upturned bills. Lateral sweeping feeding strategy.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Scolopacidae continuing from degrees 168-171. Fifth consecutive entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fifth Scolopacidae. First Limosa. Upturned bill enters -- counter-curve to curlew. Streak complete.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Each 'godwit': 0.3-0.5 seconds. Repeated 3-10+ times per sequence.
song_metrics.note_structure: Two-syllable 'godWIT!' with stress on second syllable. Repeated insistently. Clear, nasal, carrying.
song_metrics.note_types: godwit! call (primary -- flight/alarm/contact), display call (breeding -- elaborate repeated series).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Repeated two-syllable pattern: 'godWIT! godWIT! godWIT!' 3-10+ repetitions per sequence.
song_metrics.timbre: Nasal, clear, carrying. Insistent and demanding quality. Loud and projecting.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Very loud. Carries far on open tidal flats. Insistent repetition amplifies.
song_metrics.tempo: Repetitions at ~1-2 per second during alarm. Steady, insistent rhythm.
song_metrics.context: Flight, alarm, contact. Tidal flats and breeding grasslands. Given frequently.
song_metrics.key_property: REPEATED 'GODWIT!' -- loud, nasal, insistent, onomatopoeic. Upturned bill (counter-curve). Cinnamon plumage. E=5. v1.49.312.

plumage.overall: Warm cinnamon-buff throughout. Intricate barring/marbling on back and wings. Long, slightly upturned, bicolored bill (pink-orange base, dark tip). Bright cinnamon underwings in flight.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Upturned bicolored bill. Warm cinnamon plumage. Marbled back pattern. Cinnamon underwings in flight.
plumage.flight_pattern: Strong, direct. Bright cinnamon underwings diagnostic. Often calls in flight.

physical_description.overall: Length: 42-48 cm. Weight: 285-510g. Bill: 10-13 cm, slightly upturned, bicolored. Large, heavy shorebird.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Upturned bicolored bill. Warm cinnamon. Large size. Marbled pattern.
physical_description.field_id: Large, warm-toned shorebird on tidal flats. Slightly upturned, pink-based bill. Listen for insistent 'godwit! godwit!' Compare with Long-billed Curlew (down-curved bill).

nodes.title: Repeated 'Godwit!' -- The Namesake Call
nodes.description: Loud, nasal, two-syllable 'godWIT!' repeated 3-10+ times. Onomatopoeic origin. Stress on second syllable. Insistent and demanding. E=5.

nodes.title: Upturned Bill -- The Counter-Curve
nodes.description: 10-13 cm, slightly upturned. Opposite of curlew down-curve. Bicolored (pink-orange base, dark tip). Lateral sweeping feeding through soft mud and shallow water.

nodes.title: The Counter-Curve -- Bill Direction Reversal
nodes.description: After four degrees of down-curved bills (168-171), the godwit bill curves UP. Curlew down vs godwit up. Different feeding strategies accessing different prey zones. The counter-curve.

nodes.title: Curlew vs Godwit -- Opposite Curves
nodes.description: Long-billed Curlew (171): down-curved, up to 21 cm, deep probing. Marbled Godwit (172): upturned, 10-13 cm, lateral sweeping. Both cinnamon. Both on tidal flats. Opposite bill curves.

cross_references.relationship: BILL DIRECTION! Curlew DOWN (171) vs Godwit UP (172). Opposite curves. Both large, both cinnamon, both on tidal flats. Fundamental morphological contrast.
SCOLOPACIDAE STREAK! Whimbrel (170): moderate down-curve. Godwit (172): slight up-curve. The bill progression reverses between these endpoints.
SCOLOPACIDAE STREAK COMPLETE! Five consecutive (168-172). Straight > decurved > down-curved > extreme > upturned. Full bill morphology documented.
TIDAL FLAT SHARED! Both on tidal flats. Dunlin: small, in massive flocks. Godwit: large, in small groups. Different scales of tidal flat life.
